title: Hunting Errors in Application Logs
scenario: The on-call engineer received alerts about a web application returning errors. The application writes logs to three separate files inside /var/log/webapp/: access.log, error.log, and app.log. The team needs to know how many warnings were logged, which requests returned HTTP 500 errors, and whether the word 'error' appears anywhere across the full log directory.
symptoms: Web application returning errors and triggering on-call alerts
Logs distributed across access.log, error.log, and app.log in /var/log/webapp/
Unknown count of WARNING and ERROR entries
HTTP 500 failures present in the access log
mission: Use grep to search for errors and warnings across the webapp logs and save your findings to ~/errors.txt, ~/warning_count.txt, ~/all_errors.txt, and ~/server_errors.txt.

hints.title: Basic grep
hints.content: 'grep PATTERN /path/to/file' prints every line in the file that contains the pattern. By default the match is case-sensitive.

hints.title: Counting instead of printing
hints.content: Adding -c to grep makes it output only the count of matching lines, not the lines themselves. That count can then be redirected to a file.

hints.title: Case-insensitive search
hints.content: The -i flag makes grep ignore case differences. 'grep -i error' matches ERROR, Error, error, and any other capitalisation.

hints.title: Recursive search
hints.content: The -r flag tells grep to search all files inside a directory and its subdirectories. Combine it with -i for a case-insensitive recursive search.

hints.title: Bonus
hints.content: Try adding -n to any grep command. It adds line numbers to every match, which is useful when you need to tell a colleague exactly where in the file a problem appears.

acceptance_criteria.description: /home/intern/errors.txt exists and contains ERROR lines from /var/log/webapp/error.log
/home/intern/warning_count.txt exists and contains a number greater than 0
/home/intern/all_errors.txt exists and is non-empty (case-insensitive search across all files in /var/log/webapp/)
/home/intern/server_errors.txt exists and contains lines with 500 from /var/log/webapp/access.log
